body:
- Renamed `rain_particle_terminal_velocity` to `liquid_particle_terminal_velocity` since it is currently used for both cloud and rain.
- and moved it to Common to reflect it's broader applicability.
title: Refactor: liquid (cloud/rain) terminal velocity

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Use distribution quantiles to set the integration bounds for the P3 size distribution.

The existing approach was to integrate from 0 to 1, including P3 thresholds.
To achieve higher accuracy, we passed a high `order` and/or low `rtol` to `quadgk`.

This PR uses the `p`-th and `1-p`-th quantiles of the size distribution to set the integration bounds.
This is not likely more accurate, but it is more convenient and likely more computationally efficient.

I also removed type restrictions on the method signature `∫fdD_error(f, dist)`, which means that potentially other distributions in `CloudMicrophysics` could implement their own variants of `integral_bounds` (e.g. in 2M) to provide a unified interface to numerical integration.

title: refactor: set P3 integral bounds using quantiles
